entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 29 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 29 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-29 19:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • If you had a disease that could kill you, would you want to know?
  • Ben Roberts-Smith’s fate in the hands of one man
  • The Torres Strait Islander elders lawyering up to stop their homes from sinking
  • Falling enrolments, high fees threaten viability of Sydney’s Jewish schools
  • ‘It was guerrilla warfare’: Inside the fight for the future of the Australian Club
  • Anh Do shares his vision for a Marvel-style universe full of his characters
  • How the Western Bulldogs have set Ugle-Hagan up for success
  • Real-life drama behind the scenes of Aquamamma
  • ‘They might not be able to un-hear that’: When parents mess up divorce
  • Finding a nation’s Voice: The pot-holed road to referendum and reform
  • ‘They seem to have a superpower’: COVID’s super avoiders under the microscope
  • Chalmers sets large agenda as economic clouds darken
  • A tale of two treaties
  • ‘We’re all guilty of using technology as the digital babysitter’
  • Should we be worried about the spread of monkeypox?
  • Inflation rates are high - this is where Sydneysiders will see the biggest price increases
  • Would you tolerate someone refusing to play in Indigenous Round?

  • Chalmers, the kind of treasurer the Liberals longed for
  • A broken Voice is no option: Australia’s moment to unite or fail

  • ‘Epicentre’ San Francisco declares emergency over monkeypox spread
  • Spain tells women not to worry about body image on the beach - backlash ensues
  • ‘Really wounded me’: US Supreme Court Justice Alito mocks global critics of abortion ruling
  • COVID, contracts and bitter feuds: Why a Kilmore school collapsed
  • Good Weekend Superquiz and Saturday Target Time, July 30
  • We’ve become a nation of shopaholics, but can it last?
  • We’re struggling with inflation because we misread the pandemic
  • Game, set, match Cox Architecture for Rosewall Arena facelift
  • Listed property giants face acid test in reporting season
  • Hawthorn Woolworths deal shows supermarkets still market darlings
  • Neighbourhood malls in hot demand with $850m in listings

  • Origin Energy locks in coal to ease power crunch
  • Never mind home alone, interns arrive to empty offices
  • Let’s discuss turnover contagion, shift shock and great regret

  • Survey: print media portrayal of teachers doesn’t help recruitment
  • Actions have a positive impact on our school community
  • Upskilling programs provide smart start in new country
  • ‘They bit off more than they could chew’: F45 founder weighs in on turmoil

  • Russian oligarchs’ billionaire banker looks to sever his ties with the past

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 29 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Canadian court says ignoring request to wear condom violates consent
  • Russian national charged with U.S. political influence operation
  • Model says Spanish body positivity campaign used her photo without consent
  • Pope Francis ends Canada trip in Nunavut amid criticism
  • Ukraine and Russia trade blame for attack killing Mariupol prisoners
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia says Blinken call to be scheduled, ‘prisoner swap’ proposal ‘interesting’
  • Berlin’s landmarks go dark as Germany races to save energy for winter
  • Giant screen falls at concert of Hong Kong boy band Mirror, injuring 2
  • Moscow move to shutter Jewish Agency alarms Russian Jews
  • Slain journalist’s family puts spotlight on Israeli impunity
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia noncommittal on Griner deal; safe passage for grain still being arranged
  • Biden talks with China’s Xi amid tension over Taiwan
  • Pope Francis visits a Quebec that’s rapidly shedding its Catholicism
  • Human-caused climate change made U.K. heat wave 10 times more likely, study says
  • Saudi crown prince engages in long handshake with Macron on rehabilitation tour

  • Mass. just banned hair discrimination. These twins helped pave the way.
  • Private buyer spends $6.1 million on dinosaur skeleton as scientists fume
  • Siblings didn’t want mother’s killer executed. It happened anyway.

  • Man arrested with gun outside Rep. Jayapal’s home charged with stalking
  • Jan. 6 texts missing for Trump Homeland Security’s Wolf and Cuccinelli
  • FBI seeks to settle lawsuits over bungled Larry Nassar investigation
  • Post Politics Now: House to take up assault weapons ban today, Pelosi tells colleagues

  • The most Midwestern things on Earth, according to data

  • Tilden Gardens co-op apartment offered at $2.6 million

  • What’s Next for China Evergrande, Crushed by Debt?

  • Ron DeSantis is taking on woke corporations. Good.
  • Marvel comics are now Penguin Classics. Time to turn classics into Marvel.
  • Tony Dow as Wally Cleaver was a Grade A big brother
  • 2024? Biden should stay focused on the legacy he’s creating now.

  • Blaming social media for academia’s ruin misses a larger, darker truth

  • Trans people shouldn’t have to hide to help Democrats win
  • Cartoons by Drew Sheneman, Pedro X. Molina, Matt Davies and Tim Campbell
  • D.C. must do better by Black moms
  • We must help the immigrants at Union Station
  • We don’t want any missed flags in Virginia

  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 29 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 29 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-29 17:59PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘I feel unsettled’: counselling hotlines flooded with calls after accident at Mirror concert in Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong engineers say human error played part in Mirror concert tragedy, as officials place blame on show organiser
  • Hong Kong youngsters should prepare to be ambassadors to the world, Chief Executive John Lee says
  • InnoStars Awards recognise 9 for innovative inventions and work, including Hong Kong scientist who developed Covid-19 test in 3 weeks
  • Who are Mirror and how do they compare with BTS and Hong Kong’s ‘Four Heavenly Kings’ in their prime?
  • Coronavirus Hong Kong: high-risk patients aged under 60 eligible to receive Covid-19 antivirals as authorities push to reduce severe cases
  • Hong Kong national security law: 4 ex-members of defunct student activist group face up to 10 years in jail after admitting advocating for city’s ‘liberation’
  • Hair trigger: why Hong Kong schools are against schoolboys having long hair
  • ‘Abandon arrogance, face fact that national security law has restored stability in Hong Kong’: Beijing to UN Human Rights Committee on report proposing repeal of legislation
  • How much does it really cost to raise a child in Hong Kong?
  • Standard Chartered second-quarter profit beats estimates as several Asian markets rebound ‘well from the Covid-19 pandemic’
  • Accident at Mirror concert will be fully investigated, Hong Kong leader vows, as surgeons fight to help injured dancer walk again
  • Why cutting quarantine won’t be enough to revive Hong Kong’s economy: experts urge reopening of borders, full travel resumption
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong could make Covid-19 oral drugs more accessible. Here’s what you need to know about the distribution of the pills
  • No sign of Chinese build-up ahead of Nancy Pelosi’s potential Taiwan trip, White House says
  • Russia’s ‘traditional friendship’ with China remains strong, foreign minister Sergey Lavrov says
  • Alibaba to scale up cross-border e-commerce activities, engage more local exporters in joint campaign with hometown Hangzhou
  • Nancy Pelosi heads to Asia a day after Chinese leader Xi Jinping warns US not to ‘play with fire’ over Taiwan
  • Tech war: US chips act casts shadow over Beijing’s semiconductor self-sufficiency drive
  • China launches discipline probe into 3 more Henan officials as fallout from banking scam continues
  • Another heavyweight involved in China’s chip independence drive comes under official investigation: Caixin
  • Pelosi Taiwan visit: region ‘tense’ as Chinese navy watches US warships, holds drills in South China Sea
  • CanSino’s inhaled booster vaccine stronger against Omicron BA.1 subvariant than Sinovac
  • China’s new railway tunnel squeezed by tectonic crush in Tibetan snow mountain
  • Chinese, Indian foreign ministers at Shanghai bloc summit as PLA ship heads to Sri Lanka
  • Delivery firm Missfresh collapses as another Nasdaq-listed Chinese firm falls prey to weakening economy
  • China’s Politburo to ensure delivery of unfinished homes to prevent mortgage-boycott crisis from escalating
  • Former Chinese police boss Fu Zhenghua admits corruption
  • Chinese solar firms ramping up investment in Southeast Asia to evade US, European trade tensions
  • Thailand says it won’t deport Uygurs to China amid crackdown on asylum seekers
  • A year after China’s private tutoring crackdown, classes have moved underground as companies struggle to pivot
  • What did Indonesia’s Jokowi hope to achieve with his whirlwind China-Japan-South Korea tour?
  • China food security: ‘astonishing’ food loss, wastefulness worsen grain situation, state media warns
  • China’s top leadership vows to give ‘green light’ to a batch of tech deals in sign of policy relaxation
  • Hangzhou market regulator summoned Meituan, Ele.me and other food delivery platforms over poor regulation and price-cutting
  • South China Sea law enforcement ship unveiled as Beijing seeks to tighten control over disputed waters
  • As chip-making business returns to US, Washington – and Arizona – dangle subsidies in race against China
  • US House approves Chips Act to subsidise and speed up semiconductor production
  • US scrutiny of solar products from China’s Xinjiang could ‘create opportunities’ for Indian manufacturers
  • Glass half empty: Why Japan’s love affair with Beaujolais Nouveau has soured
  • Philippines detects first case of monkeypox
  • Singapore, New York see highest jump in rental prices this year, while Hong Kong sees decline
  • US, Japan discuss supply chains, semiconductors in ‘2-plus-2’ talks in face of China’s rise and Russia’s war
  • Australian TV soap ‘Neighbours’ that gave starts to Margo Robbie, Kylie Minogue, Russel Crowe, and Chris Hemsworth signs off after 37 years
  • South Korea steps closer to Ukraine war with massive arms deal to Poland
  • 16 dead in ‘devastating’ Kentucky flooding with death toll expected to double, says state governor
  • Will Smith posts apology video for slapping Chris Rock at Oscars ceremony in March
  • Elon Musk-Twitter trial over US$44 billion deal to start on October 17
  • Ukraine war: US vowed to isolate Russia but Putin’s jet-setting shows it is hard to do
  • Coleen Rooney wins ‘Wagatha Christie’ libel case against Rebekah Vardy
  • Spain prosecutors seek 8-year jail term for singer Shakira over tax fraud
  • Russia says 40 Ukrainian POWs killed in Kyiv missile strike on Donetsk prison
  • Russia tasking mercenaries for front line as infantry losses in Ukraine mount, UK says
  • Joe Biden shrugs off recession talk, declares US ‘on the right path’
  • Pope Francis seeks forgiveness in Canada for sex abuse at residential schools
  • US Supreme Court Justice Alito mocks foreign critics in first public remarks since abortion ruling
  • First kisses may have helped spread cold sore virus
  • California court upholds death penalty for Hong Kong-born serial killer Charles Ng in 1980s sex slave murders
  • Russian journalist in TV protest fined again for discrediting army over Ukraine
  • Joe Biden is suddenly winning (just don’t say the ‘R’ word)
  • France’s Macron defies anger to welcome Saudi crown prince
  • From China to the US, spending on healthcare, housing and education must rise
  • KFC, Pizza Hut operator Yum China reports 54 per cent profit drop after Covid-19 curbs kept diners at bay
  • Global investments in sustainable funds plummet amid fears of looming recession, Morningstar says
  • Exchange Fund, Hong Kong’s currency-peg defender, suffers US$18.4 billion record loss on ‘perfect storm’ of war, disease and slumping markets
  • Threat of ‘heatflation’ looms large as climate change shrinks farm and seafood output, experts say
  • Alibaba, Meituan, Tencent sink Hong Kong stocks on regulatory pressure while China sticks to zero-Covid, slow-drip stimulus
  • Ancient tree in Netflix K-drama Extraordinary Attorney Woo draws tourists to a small village in South Korea
  • K-drama midseason recap: Cafe Minamdang – Netflix’s dodgy shaman-themed crime comedy, starring Seo In-guk and Oh Yeon-seo, sidelines its best characters
  • Japanese rail company asks passengers to roll the dice with its tickets to random destinations
  • 4 ways to pick a good wine: leave your usual chardonnay and sauvignon blanc and drink something different
  • Jazz, spiritual and other musical styles blended with Bach to complete Baroque maestro’s Little Organ Book, left unfinished when he died
  • How Hong Kong teachers’ mental health suffered from Covid-19 school closures, which caused 70pc to consider leaving teaching, new study finds
  • ‘The best saxophonist in Hong Kong’: jazz album by Timothy Sun showcases music by young composers in the city
  • Taron Egerton’s gritty role in Black Bird on Apple TV+ is worlds away from his turn as Elton John in Rocketman and a suave spy in Kingsman
  • Hotpot card game that plays on Cantonese slang and memes and the creators behind it, whose goal is to help preserve Hong Kong culture
  • A viral TikTok on why Stranger Things star should have Botox backfires – fans call nurse’s suggestions to Natalia Dyer ‘weird’
  • 12 richest NBA players of 2022 – net worths, ranked: from billionaires LeBron James and Michael Jordan and their Nike deals, to Shaquille O’Neal and Magic Johnson – but who became a Coca-Cola CEO?
  • Who was Colonel Tom Parker, Elvis Presley’s manager who allegedly stole over half of his millions? Inside their controversial connection, depicted in Baz Luhrmann’s new biopic
  • Where is Hunter Moore’s ex-girlfriend Kirra Hughes now? The Most Hated Man on the Internet docu showed her side of the story – now she’s modelling, Instagramming and living with a new love
  • Where is Hunter Moore, The Most Hated Man on the Internet, now? Netflix’s latest villain was released from jail in 2017, sold website Is Anyone Up? and has written a book – but he’s still not sorry
  • Why this Taiwan icon is next on our travel bucket list: the avant garde Taipei Performing Arts Centre could join monuments across the globe as a not-to-be-missed architectural attraction
  • 6 K-pop idols with tattoos who aren’t afraid to flaunt them, from BTS’ Jungkook, Got7’s Jackson Wang and Twice’s Chaeyoung, to Jay Park, Hyolyn and Hyuna – who matches with her fiancé Dawn
  • Meet new K-pop girl band NewJeans: Minji was in a BTS’ MV with Ed Sheeran, Haerin has been compared to Blackpink’s Jennie, and Hyein loves Harry Potter … but what about Hanni and Danielle?
  • STYLE Edit: Anna Hu’s debut at The European Fine Art Foundation (TEFAF) Maastricht – the Taiwan-born designer showcased dazzling art deco, flowers and music-themed high jewellery at the fine art fair
  • Would you splurge millions to live in a palace? Castles for sale in the Netherlands, Scotland and France, complete with moats and turrets to make you feel like a true royal
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
